story_clusters.summary: Tripura and Bombay High Courts ruled in favor of taxpayers in separate GST cases: the Tripura HC allowed a contractor’s GST reimbursement under a contract clause despite a missing Schedule A, while the Bombay HC set aside a refund rejection for violating Rule 92(3) procedure. Additionally, the Bombay HC held employees not liable for GST penalties due to lack of taxable person status, and the Andhra Pradesh HC ruled that an SGST officer lacked jurisdiction over IGST on imports, as only customs authorities could assess such taxes.

story_clusters.summary: Multiple exit polls for West Bengal’s 2026 elections predict a competitive race between the BJP and TMC. CNN-News18’s Vote Vibe projects the BJP ahead with 143–163 seats and the TMC at 127–147, while Today’s Chanakya forecasts a larger BJP victory with 192 seats. Some polls suggest a potential cliffhanger between the two parties.

story_clusters.summary: India is preparing to notify eased foreign direct investment (FDI) norms that would allow firms with up to 10% Chinese ownership to access the automatic route, though the final notification is still pending. The changes aim to ease restrictions for investors from countries sharing a land border with India, including China. The government approved the amendments 50 days prior, but the formal rollout has yet to occur.

story_clusters.summary: The Indian rupee has reached record lows, prompting concerns about India’s cheap-money era and potential shifts in Reserve Bank of India (RBI) policy. The RBI has intervened to stabilize the currency amid oil-related pressures, while also launching surveys to gather input for monetary policy decisions. Additionally, the RBI has provided FAQs on the Digital Rupee (e₹) as part of its digital currency initiatives.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has issued new rules for 2026 regarding agency bank commissions and oversight, while also implementing e-mandate changes to streamline high-value payments. Additionally, RBI data shows bank credit growth slowed to 14.88% in the first half of April, with industry credit expanding at a pace of 15% in March.

story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court has reserved its judgment on Congress leader Pawan Khera's plea for anticipatory bail in an Assam Police FIR. story_clusters.summary: The Railway Minister flagged off the Jammu Tawi to Srinagar Vande Bharat Express, with regular services set to begin on May 2. Additionally, the Railway Minister announced plans for a future rail link to Uri.

story_clusters.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi inaugurated the ₹36,000 crore Ganga Expressway, a 594-kilometer-long expressway connecting Meerut to Prayagraj in Uttar Pradesh. The project spans 12 districts and is expected to reduce travel time between Meerut and Prayagraj. The expressway has been developed as an industrial corridor to boost regional connectivity and economic growth.

articles.title: Loose Sheets and Digital Data Not Enough: ITAT Quashes Additions for Lack of Evidence
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that tax authorities cannot make additions to a taxpayer's income based solely on loose sheets or digital data seized from third parties unless there is a clear link to the assessee. The tribunal emphasized that third-party documents without corroboration lack evidentiary value. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle evidence in assessments and may influence future tax policy and enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that additions cannot stand without a clear link between seized material and the assessee. It ruled that third-party documents without corroboration lack evidentiary value. The post Loose Sheets and Digital Data Not Enough: ITAT Quashes Additions for Lack of Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru .

The SC's ruling supports the product's treatment as a mosquito repellent for customs and regulatory purposes.

articles.title: Govt lowers export duty on diesel, ATF; petrol duty unchanged
articles.summary: The Indian government has reduced the export duty on diesel and aviation turbine fuel (ATF) while keeping the duty on petrol unchanged. Additionally, new regulations now permit the blending of ATF with synthetic fuels, expanding the definition of aviation fuel to align with international standards. These changes aim to adjust fuel pricing and adapt to evolving aviation industry practices.
articles.raw_summary: Separately, new rules allow blending of ATF with synthetic fuels, expanding fuel definitions to align with evolving aviation standards.

articles.summary: New Reserve Bank of India guidelines empower banks to offer relief to all borrowers in calamity-hit regions. These measures, effective July 1, 2026, can be extended without borrower requests. Banks can also operate from temporary premises and set up satellite offices. ATM services will be restored promptly. Relief can include fee waivers for up to a year.

articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a penalty notice issued without a proper signature is legally invalid and cannot establish jurisdiction. This decision underscores the mandatory requirement for authenticated notices in tax penalty proceedings. The case involved a challenge to a penalty imposed under Section 271(1)(c) of the Income Tax Act.

articles.title: Why Asia Cannot Replace Gulf Crude Easily

The rise of infrastructure for digital censorshipA series of IT Rules amendments has shifted online speech control from courts to the executive, enabling fast, opaque takedowns with limited accountability.12 April, 20266 min readApar GuptaNikhil Pahwa
articles.summary: A series of amendments to India's IT Rules has transferred control over online speech from the judiciary to the executive branch. This change allows for rapid, opaque content takedowns with minimal oversight. The Internet Freedom Foundation highlights concerns over accountability and free speech implications. The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

The new system aims to prevent forgery, alterations, and manual errors that were common in the old method. This initiative is part of broader efforts to modernize land records and improve transparency in property transactions.

articles.title: How a law to empower Adivasis is being used to target Christians in Chhattisgarh
articles.summary: The Panchayats (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act (PESA) in Chhattisgarh, designed to empower Adivasis, is being misused to target Christians. While land rights provisions of PESA are rarely enforced, its cultural protection clauses are invoked to ostracize Christian communities. This misuse raises concerns about the law's implementation and its impact on minority rights in tribal areas.
articles.raw_summary: PESA provisions about land rights are hardly used, many say. But its clauses about cultural protections are invoked to ostracise those who follow Christianity.

articles.title: Mandatory PAN Requirement Introduced for Post Office Transactions Under Income Tax Rules 2026
articles.summary: The Indian government will require a Permanent Account Number (PAN) for all post office transactions starting in 2026 under updated Income Tax Rules. This change, introduced through Form 121, aims to strengthen documentation and reporting for tax purposes. The move emphasizes greater accountability and structured tax compliance mechanisms for financial transactions conducted at post offices.

This allowed the exemption under Section 11 to stand. The case clarifies legal interpretations of tax exemptions for trusts in India.

articles.title: Govt reduces windfall gains tax on diesel, jet fuel
articles.summary: The Indian government has reduced the Special Additional Excise Duty (SAED) on diesel exports from ₹55.5 per litre to ₹23 per litre and on aviation turbine fuel from ₹42 per litre to ₹33 per litre. This move aims to ease export costs for these fuels. The decision is part of the central government's fiscal policy adjustments to address windfall gains in the energy sector.

articles.title: ITAT Rejects Appeal Where Declared Capital Gains Accepted Without Adjustment
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) dismissed an appeal where the declared capital gains were accepted by the Assessing Officer without adjustments. The Tribunal ruled that since no additions were made after verification, the appeal lacked merit and was infructuous. The decision underscores the importance of tax compliance and the limited scope for appeals when disclosures are accepted without challenge.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that since the Assessing Officer made no addition after verifying disclosures, the grievance lacked merit. articles.title: Environment ministry writes to states about penalties for illegal use of forest land
articles.summary: The Union Environment Ministry sent a letter to state governments on Monday, highlighting that states are submitting incomplete undertakings from agencies violating forest land use rules instead of full details as required by guidelines. The ministry emphasized the need for strict compliance with the Forest Conservation Act (FCA) to penalize illegal activities. This action aims to strengthen enforcement against unauthorized forest land use across states.
articles.raw_summary: The letter, issued on Monday, said the states are sending just undertakings from violating agencies, instead of furnishing complete details of the PCA as required in the guidelines.

articles.title: Reopening Invalid as Notice Issued Beyond Six-Year Limitation Period: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that tax reassessment notices issued after a six-year limitation period are invalid and time-barred. The Court upheld that such notices cannot be legally sustained, setting a precedent for future tax reassessment cases. This decision impacts taxpayers and tax authorities by clarifying the time limits for issuing reassessment notices under Indian tax law.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether reassessment notice issued after six years was valid. The Court upheld that such notices are time-barred and cannot be sustained under law. The post Reopening Invalid as Notice Issued Beyond Six-Year Limitation Period: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

The dispute highlights political tensions around women's quota policies and parliamentary procedures. The event reflects broader debates on gender representation in governance.

articles.title: Employees Not Liable for GST Penalty in Absence of Taxable Person Status: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that employees cannot be penalized under Section 122(1A) of the GST Act for company-level violations unless they are registered as 'taxable persons.' The court held that only those with independent GST registration can be held liable, excluding regular employees. This decision clarifies the scope of liability for GST violations and impacts how companies and employees may be treated in such cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether employees could be penalised under Section 122(1A) for company-level GST violations. It held that only “taxable persons” fall within the scope of the provision, excluding employees without independent registration. The post Employees Not Liable for GST Penalty in Absence of Taxable Person Status: Bombay HC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: The Gold Monetisation Scheme 2026 allows households to deposit idle gold with banks or institutions to earn interest without selling it. articles.title: Kerala exit poll: Congress-led UDF edges ahead of Left, race tight but power may be shifting
articles.summary: Exit poll projections suggest the Congress-led United Democratic Front (UDF) is leading in Kerala with 70–80 seats in the 140-member Assembly, potentially crossing the majority mark. The Left Democratic Front (LDF) is projected at 58–68 seats, while the National Democratic Alliance (NDA) is estimated to win 0–4 seats. The tight race indicates a possible shift in power dynamics that could impact future state policies.
articles.raw_summary: Seat projections place the UDF at 70–80 seats, with a midpoint of 75, above the majority mark of 71 in the 140 member Assembly. The LDF is projected at 58–68 seats, while the NDA is estimated at 0–4 seats, indicating limited seat conversion.

articles.summary: The court ruled that a lower Tax Deduction at Source (TDS) certificate issued under Section 197 of the Income Tax Act applies for the entire assessment year, not just prospectively. This decision negates any default or interest liability for the taxpayer. The ruling clarifies the scope of TDS certificates, impacting tax compliance and administrative practices.

articles.title: Patna HC Sets Aside POCSO Conviction, Rules Expression “Bad Work” is Insufficient to Presume Penetrative Sexual Assault
articles.summary: The Patna High Court overturned a POCSO (Protection of Children from Sexual Offences) conviction, stating that the phrase 'bad work' and the presence of semen on the victim's clothing were insufficient evidence to presume penetrative sexual assault. The court emphasized that such assumptions cannot be made without stronger proof. This ruling impacts how POCSO cases are adjudicated and sets a legal precedent for future cases involving child sexual abuse.
articles.raw_summary: Merely on the presence of semen on the pant of the victim and on the basis of the ocular evidence that some  bad work  was done, it cannot be presumed that penetrative sexual assault to some extent was committed upon her. The central bank has already repatriated a large portion of its gold. This action follows global events that raised concerns about storing sovereign assets abroad. Many countries are now repatriating their gold.

Many countries are now repatriating their gold.

articles.title: Your Gold Bond Tax Break Is Gone, Here’s What It Actually Costs You
articles.summary: The government has removed the tax break for secondary buyers of Sovereign Gold Bonds (SGBs) by imposing a 12.5% Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) tax. This change applies only to secondary market buyers, unlike primary holders who remain exempt. The policy shift alters the returns and investment strategies for gold bonds, impacting investors and the market. The decision was implemented through a regulatory change affecting tax treatment of SGB transactions.
articles.raw_summary: Secondary SGB buyers must now pay 12.5% LTCG tax, unlike primary holders. The change reshapes returns and investment strategies in gold bonds. The post Your Gold Bond Tax Break Is Gone, Here s What It Actually Costs You appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.summary: Karnataka's state government plans to develop 1,000 MW of data park capacity across Bengaluru, Mysuru, and Mangaluru to boost industrial growth. The state government will ensure uninterrupted power and water supply for investors to attract digital infrastructure projects.

articles.title: The Plant Fibre Sector Keeps Chasing Visibility When It Needs Markets
articles.summary: The plant fibre sector in India is gaining attention as industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact. However, output remains low due to fragmented supply chains, weak processing capacity, and uncertain demand. A FIBRAL report suggests that diversification will require standards, investment, and market architecture, not just increased fibre production. The sector's struggles highlight potential areas for policy intervention to boost sustainable alternatives.
articles.raw_summary: As industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact, alternative plant fibres are drawing new policy and commercial attention. Yet output remains marginal and forecast to contract, constrained by fragmented chains, weak processing capacity and uncertain offtake. A FIBRAL report argues diversification will depend on standards, investment and market architecture, not fibre discovery alone at scale.

articles.title: Lok Adalat in Noida to hear pending cases on May 9: Here’s all you need to know
articles.summary: A Lok Adalat (people's court) session will be held in Noida on May 9 at 10:00 AM across multiple court locations, including the District Court in Surajpur. Organized by the judiciary, these sessions aim to resolve pending cases out of court. Lok Adalats are a formal dispute resolution mechanism under Indian law, providing a faster and less adversarial alternative to traditional court proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: The session will begin at 10:00 AM and will be organised at headquarters and Tehsil courts, including the District Court in Surajpur.

The regulator shared these findings with the European Commission, suggesting systemic issues in compliance. This highlights broader concerns about product safety and regulation in digital marketplaces, which could influence India's approach to e-commerce and consumer protection policies.
articles.raw_summary: France's consumer watchdog found a staggering 75% of products from online platforms failed EU safety rules. Many items were found to be dangerous, posing risks like electric shock or containing harmful chemicals. This widespread non-compliance suggests systemic issues rather than isolated incidents. The findings will be shared with the European Commission for potential action against platforms.

articles.title: MoSPI proposes GST-based index to track services sector, seeks feedback
articles.summary: The Ministry of Statistics and Programme Implementation (MoSPI) has proposed creating a new Index of Service Production using aggregated GST data to monitor India's services sector. The proposal seeks feedback from stakeholders on its methodology. This initiative aims to improve the tracking and analysis of the services sector's performance, which is a key component of India's economy.

articles.title: Bengal polls: EC asks police to conduct special drive after explosives seized ahead of second phase
articles.summary: The Election Commission (EC) has directed police to conduct a special drive after authorities seized 79 crude bomb-like objects, 3.3 kg of gunpowder, and 1.6 kg of sulphur ahead of the second phase of the Bengal polls. The seizure highlights security concerns during the election process. The EC's directive aims to ensure safety and prevent potential disruptions in the electoral process.

The analysis highlights potential challenges for India's inflation management and
articles.raw_summary: Jahangir Aziz, Co-head of Macroeconomic Research at JPMorgan, pushes back against expectations of imminent rate cuts, arguing that persistent inflation risks and stable labour markets leave little room for easing in 2026. The bank will support the region's economy while monitoring fast-moving risks.

The ADB warns of systemic, long-lasting disruptions to global energy and trade networks. The bank will support the region's economy and track fast-moving risks.

articles.title: Rising illegal wildlife trade across Himalayan countries threatens the mountain ecosystem
articles.summary: In February, forest rangers and officials in Himachal Pradesh raided jewelry shops in Rohru as part of 'Operation Clawing Back' to seize jewelry allegedly made from leopard claws and teeth. This incident reflects a broader issue of illegal wildlife trade across Himalayan countries, threatening mountain ecosystems. Such crimes highlight gaps in wildlife protection laws and enforcement, raising concerns about biodiversity conservation and potential policy responses.

articles.title: Better preparedness and warning systems needed to deal with ice-rock avalanches
articles.summary: Five years after the 2021 Uttarakhand floods (Chamoli disaster), a new study examines hazard preparedness in the Himalayas. It compares the 2021 disaster with the 2025 Blatten avalanche in Switzerland, where an ice-rock avalanche buried a village. The study emphasizes the need for improved warning systems and preparedness to mitigate such risks in vulnerable regions like the Himalayas.
articles.raw_summary: Five years after the 2021 floods in Uttarakhand, known as the Chamoli disaster, that resulted in the death of more than 200 people, a new study investigates hazard preparedness in the Himalayas. It compares the 2021 Chamoli disaster with the 2025 Blatten avalanche in Switzerland, where a large ice-rock avalanche buried most of the village [ ]

articles.title: India’s rare swamp deer establishes a second home
articles.summary: A population of hard-ground swamp deer translocated from Kanha Tiger Reserve to Satpura Tiger Reserve, both in Madhya Pradesh, is growing steadily and breeding successfully, according to a new study. articles.title: Plastic litter and ghost nets threaten olive ridley hatchlings
articles.summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha is a critical nesting site for olive ridley sea turtles, with nearly 700,000 turtles arriving in February 2025 for mass nesting. Plastic waste and discarded fishing nets (ghost nets) pose severe threats to hatchlings, risking their survival. The article underscores the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect this endangered species and its habitat.
articles.raw_summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha’s Ganjam district is among the most important turtle rookeries in the world. Every year, hundreds of thousands of olive ridley sea turtles (Lepidochelys olivacea) converge here to lay eggs in a synchronous mass nesting event or arribada, with almost 700,000 olive ridleys coming ashore to lay eggs in February 2025 alone. articles.title: India industrial output at 4.1%, hit by Iran war
articles.summary: India's industrial output grew 4.1% in March, a five-month low. This slowdown was influenced by weaker electricity and manufacturing sectors. Economists noted the impact of the Iran conflict on supplies and costs. Despite the moderation, growth surpassed expectations. Manufacturing saw a dip, though capital goods showed strong investment demand. Government spending supported infrastructure growth.

Manufacturing saw a dip, though capital goods showed strong investment demand. Government spending supported infrastructure growth.

articles.title: Industrial growth dips to five-month low of 4.1% in March
articles.summary: India’s industrial output growth slowed to a five-month low of 4.1% in March from 5.1% in February, weighed down by weaker manufacturing and electricity output, amid supply disruptions and rising costs linked to the Iran conflict.

articles.title: Affluent India shifts to experience-led spending, travel accounts for 58% of discretionary wallet: Report
articles.summary: India's wealthy are spending more on experiences, especially travel. This shift means consumption is more about personal identity than just buying things. The affluent population is growing, and spending is spreading beyond big cities. Travel, retail, and luxury are key areas. Dining out is also a major focus for affluent Indians. Luxury is moving towards access rather than ownership.

Luxury is moving towards access rather than ownership.

articles.title: India industrial output hits 5-month low of 4.1% in March as power, manufacturing drag
articles.summary: India's factory output saw a slight increase in March 2026, reaching 4.1 percent. Manufacturing and mining sectors showed positive growth. Electricity generation, however, lagged behind. This steady momentum in industrial activity signals a stable economic outlook for the upcoming fiscal year. The Index of Industrial Production remains a crucial indicator of economic health.

articles.title: Gold heads for weekly loss amid oil-driven inflation concerns
articles.summary: Gold prices held steady Friday, but a weekly dip was likely as soaring oil prices fueled inflation concerns, impacting interest rate expectations. Meanwhile, geopolitical tensions in the Strait of Hormuz and a significant drop in India's gold imports due to tax issues also played a role in the market's movements.

articles.title: RBI's net short positions hit a record $104 billion
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's net short forward position surged to a record $104 billion in March, a significant increase from February's $77 billion. This reflects the RBI's continuous intervention to protect the rupee against volatility, impacting India's import cover to under nine months.

articles.summary: Heavy rains in Bengaluru flooded streets, uprooted trees, killed 10 people, and caused power cuts across large parts of the city. The downpour disrupted normal life in key areas, highlighting vulnerabilities in urban infrastructure and emergency response systems. The incident underscores the need for better drainage systems, climate resilience planning, and disaster preparedness in rapidly growing cities.
articles.raw_summary: The intense downpour flooded hundreds of streets, uprooted trees, claimed lives, out power across large swathes of the city and disrupted normal life across several key areas.
